verification_performed: Read core/types.py to confirm inning is carried as `inning` on the game/signal context path
Read the bridge loop in bot_core.py to confirm `intent.get("inning")` is available before `check_entry_gates()`
Checked risk.py and confirmed LATE_INNING_BLOCK was not already read there
Implemented Option A in bot_core.py as a pre-gate bridge rejection before `check_entry_gates()`
observed: `intent.get("inning")` is already present in the active bridge loop before the live gate call, so the inning value is available without widening the `check_entry_gates()` signature.
risk.py did not read LATE_INNING_BLOCK and did not contain inning-based blocking logic.
The smallest active enforcement point is the bridge loop in bot_core.py, immediately before `check_entry_gates()` is called.
proven: Chosen option: Option A, pre-gate check in bot_core.py before `check_entry_gates()`.
Justification: this is the narrowest fix because inning is already present on the intent object at the call site, so no risk.py signature change was needed and no broader gate plumbing was required.
Implementation added a module-level `LATE_INNING_BLOCK = int(os.getenv("LATE_INNING_BLOCK", "7"))` in bot_core.py.

Implementation added a pre-gate check that parses `intent.get("inning")`, and when `inning >= LATE_INNING_BLOCK`, logs `BRIDGE GATE REJECT [late_inning_block] slug=%s reason=inning=%s>=%s` and skips the entry.

likely: This is the safest narrow fix for the confirmed wiring gap because it touches only the active live bridge path and does not alter the broader shared risk gate contract.
A restart is required before live runtime will actually enforce this new bridge-side late-inning block.
ruled_out: Ruled out: Option B being the smaller change. It would require modifying the shared `check_entry_gates()` interface and call contract for information that is already available at the bridge call site.
Ruled out: any need to modify core/types.py or risk.py for this narrow wiring fix.
